'''Tafeaga Junior Sa'u''' (born 18 April 1987) is a professional New Zealand and Samoan [[rugby league]] player who plays for [[Rochdale Hornets]].

He previously played for [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] and [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] at the international level.

He previously played for the [[Newcastle Knights]] and the [[Melbourne Storm]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]], the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]], and the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[Super League]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> He spent time in 2019 on loan from [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] at [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the Super League. Sa'u has also played rugby union for [[Old Glory DC]] in [[Major League Rugby]] (MLR) in the United States.

==Background==
Sa'u was born in [[Auckland, New Zealand]] to Samoan parents from [[Lauli'i]] and [[Apolima]].<ref name=Tafeaga />

He played his junior football for [[Logan Brothers Rugby League Football Club|Logan Brothers]] in [[Queensland]], Australia before being signed by the [[Canberra Raiders]].

==Playing career==
===Early career===
He played for Canberra's [[NSW Cup|Premier League]] team in 2006, scoring seven tries.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.rleague.com//db/player/s/sau_junior/index.php |title=Junior SA'U |publisher=Rugby League Project |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120617155757/http://www.rleague.com//db/player/s/sau_junior/index.php |archive-date=17 June 2012}}</ref>

===Newcastle Knights===
Sa'u then signed a four-year contract starting in 2008 to be a part of the newly cleaned out [[Newcastle Knights]].
 
In Round 13 of the [[2008 NRL season]] he made his [[NRL]] début for the Newcastle club against the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]]. He scored two tries on debut. 
[[File:Junior Sau.jpg|thumb|right|Sa'u playing for the Newcastle Knights in 2009]]
In June 2011, Sa'u re-signed with the Newcastle club for a further two years.<ref>{{cite news| url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/cowboys-lifeline-for-paterson-20110601-1fgpm.html| work=The Sydney Morning Herald| title=Cowboys' lifeline for Paterson}}</ref>

===Melbourne Storm===
On 18 August 2012, Sa'u was released from the final year of his Newcastle contract and signed with the [[Melbourne Storm]] on a one-year contract starting in 2013.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.melbournestorm.com.au/news-display/Sau-signs-with-Storm/61963 |title=Sa'u signs with Storm |access-date=2012-11-18 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130517073742/http://www.melbournestorm.com.au/news-display/Sau-signs-with-Storm/61963 |archive-date=17 May 2013 }}</ref>

===Salford Red Devils===
In June 2013, it was announced that Sa'u had signed a two-year deal with [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] in the Super League starting in 2014.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/salford-add-dash-hot-sauce-4708079 |title=Salford add a dash of Hot Sauce with Junior Sa'u |publisher=Manchester Evening News |date=25 June 2013 |access-date=28 May 2022}}</ref>

Scoring 12 tries in his début season with Salford, he was voted k'Supporter's Player of the Year' for 2014.

===Wakefield Trinity===
On 14 May 2019, after five years with the Salford club, Sau joined fellow [[Super League]] team [[Wakefield Trinity]] on an initial one month loan.

===Leigh Centurions===
Sa'u joined the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[2019 RFL Championship]].

In round 18 of the [[2021 Super League season]], he scored two tries for Leigh in a 28-34 loss against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58138787|title=Super League: Leigh Centurions 28-34 Hull Kingston Rovers - visitors edge 62-point thriller|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>

===Old Glory DC===
Sa'u made the switch to [[rugby union]] in 2022, joining [[Major League Rugby]] side [[Old Glory DC]] in the United States.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.americasrugbynews.com/2021/10/16/old-glory-signs-rugby-league-international-junior-sau/|title = Old Glory signs rugby league international Junior Sa'u|publisher=Americas Rugby News|date=16 October 2021|access-date=18 March 2022}}</ref>

===Keighley Cougars===
At the conclusion of the [[2022 Major League Rugby season]] Sa'u returned to the United Kingdom and rugby league and signed for League 1 team [[Keighley Cougars]] until the end of the [[2022 RFL League 1|2022 League 1 season]].<ref>{{cite web |last1=Gordon |first1=James |title=Junior Sa'u makes move to League One leaders |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/junior-sau-makes-move-to-league-one-leaders/ |website=LoveRugbyLeague |access-date=9 June 2022 |date=9 June 2022}}</ref> Sa'u was named as captain of the team for the 2025 season.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Chalk |first=Ethan Lee |date=2024-12-25 |title=Former Knights centre appointed captain of overseas team |url=https://www.zerotackle.com/former-knights-centre-appointed-captain-of-overseas-team-217918/ |access-date=2024-12-26 |website=Zero Tackle |language=en-US}}</ref>

===Rochdale Hornets===
On 1 October 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Rochdale Hornets]] for 2026<ref>{{cite web |author=Ben Olawumi|title=Veteran ex-NRL and Super League stalwart makes League 1 switch for 2026 |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/junior-sau-makes-league-1-switch-for-2026|website=LoveRugbyLeague |date=1 Oct 2025}}</ref>

==International career==
In 2008, Sa'u was named in the [[Samoa national rugby league team#2008 World Cup squad|Samoa training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]] but did not make the final squad.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.leagueunlimited.com/article.php?newsid=16750 |title=Samoa name World Cup Squad |publisher=League Unlimited |date=2008-08-05 |access-date=2008-08-06 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110717060051/http://www.leagueunlimited.com/article.php?newsid=16750 |archive-date=17 July 2011 }}</ref>

In 2009, Sa'u was selected in the [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand Kiwis]] [[2009 Rugby League Four Nations|Four Nations]] squad and made his international début for the Kiwis against [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]].<ref name="NZ_Herald_10602715">{{cite news|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=10602715&ref=rss|title=League: Chance for rookie Kiwis|date=12 October 2009|agency= [[NZPA]]|work= [[The New Zealand Herald]]|access-date=8 October 2011}}</ref>

In 2010, Sa'u was selected for [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] in the [[2010 ANZAC Test]]. He was also again selected in the New Zealand squad for the [[2010 Rugby League Four Nations|2010 Four Nations]].

In 2013 Junior played for [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] in the Pacific Rugby League International against [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]]. He was also called in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]] squad to replace the initially selected Roy Asotasi.

==Personal life==
He is a cousin to former [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] player [[Steve Matai]].

In 2009, Sa'u was praised for coming to the aid of a 15-year-old girl who was being assaulted in [[Waratah, New South Wales]].<ref>{{cite news|author = Nick Walshaw|publisher=Fox Sports News (Australia)|title=Newcastle Knights' Junior Sau hailed for saving girl's life|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/league/nrl-premiership/newcastle-knights-junior-sau-hailed-for-saving-girls-life/story-fn2mcuj6-1225744546227|access-date=7 August 2009|date=1 July 2009|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120921083158/http://www.foxsports.com.au/league/nrl-premiership/newcastle-knights-junior-sau-hailed-for-saving-girls-life/story-fn2mcuj6-1225744546227 |archive-date=21 September 2012 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
